overview.description: Hackensack is the county seat of Bergen County and one of northern New Jersey's most important urban centers. Home to the renowned Hackensack University Medical Center and the Bergen County Courthouse, it serves as a hub for government, healthcare, commerce, and culture. The city has undergone significant revitalization in recent years, with new residential developments, dining establishments, and cultural venues transforming its downtown into a walkable, vibrant urban core.
highlights: County seat of Bergen County — home to the courthouse and county government offices
Hackensack University Medical Center — one of NJ's premier hospitals and largest employers
Major downtown revitalization with new mixed-use residential and retail developments
Historic Main Street with diverse dining, shopping, and cultural offerings
Hackensack River Greenway and waterfront park development underway

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Properties are assessed by the City Tax Assessor. Hackensack has a diverse housing stock ranging from pre-war single-family homes to modern luxury apartments. PILOT (Payment in Lieu of Taxes) programs apply to several new developments.
realEstate.recentTrends: Hackensack's real estate market has been transformed by downtown redevelopment. New luxury apartment buildings have attracted young professionals and empty nesters. Single-family home prices have risen steadily. The city offers relative value compared to neighboring suburbs.
neighborhoodNotes: Downtown / Main Street area features new luxury apartments and mixed-use developments with walkable amenities
Fairmount section in the south is a quiet residential area with single-family homes and tree-lined streets
Prospect Avenue / Hospital area has a mix of housing types near Hackensack University Medical Center
Johnson Avenue corridor in the north includes diverse neighborhoods with more affordable housing options
Hackensack River waterfront is an emerging area with planned parks and residential development

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash collection is provided twice weekly by the City's Department of Public Works.
details: Collection days vary by ward — check the city website for your schedule
Trash must be in sealed bags or covered containers, maximum 50 lbs
Place at curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Bulk item pickup available by calling the DPW to schedule
No hazardous waste, electronics, or tires in regular trash

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected weekly, managed through the Bergen County Utilities Authority program.
details: Commingled recyclables (glass, plastic #1-#7, aluminum, steel cans) in blue bin
Paper, cardboard, and newspapers collected separately
No plastic bags, Styrofoam, or food-contaminated items
City recycling drop-off center available at the DPW yard

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water is provided by Veolia Water (formerly United Water/Suez). Sewer service is managed by the city and processed through the Bergen County Utilities Authority.
details: Water bills issued quarterly by Veolia
Sewer charges included in property tax bills
Water quality meets all federal and state standards
Backflow prevention devices may be required for certain properties

permits.title: Building Permits
permits.description: The Building Department manages all construction permits, inspections, and code enforcement for the City of Hackensack.
details: Permits required for construction, renovation, demolition, and changes of use
Zoning Board and Planning Board meet regularly to review applications
Hackensack has specific redevelopment zones with different requirements
Fire safety permits required for commercial properties
Applications available at City Hall or online

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: Hackensack operates multiple municipal parking garages, surface lots, and metered street parking throughout the downtown area.
details: Municipal parking garages at River Street and Atlantic Street
Metered street parking on Main Street and surrounding blocks
ParkMobile app accepted at most meters and garages
Monthly parking permits available for commuters and downtown workers
Free parking on Sundays and city holidays

additionalServices.title: Senior Services
additionalServices.description: The Hackensack Senior Center provides programs, meals, transportation, and social services for residents aged 60+.
details: Located at the Hackensack Community Center
Daily congregate meals and Meals on Wheels program
Transportation for medical appointments and errands
Exercise, arts, and educational programs
Tax preparation assistance during tax season

additionalServices.title: Library Services
additionalServices.description: The Johnson Free Public Library serves as the city's main library, offering extensive collections and programs.
details: Located at 274 Main Street in downtown Hackensack
Free Wi-Fi, computer access, and printing
Children's story times and teen programs
ESL classes and citizenship preparation
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Hackensack enforces noise regulations to balance its urban commercial activity with residential quality of life.
keyRules: Construction work permitted Monday-Friday 7:00 AM to 7:00 PM
Saturday construction 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction on Sundays or holidays
Amplified music and sound from commercial establishments restricted after 10:00 PM
Vehicle noise (horns, modified exhausts, boom boxes) subject to fines

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: The city actively enforces property maintenance standards as part of its revitalization efforts.
keyRules: Lawns and yards must be maintained — grass exceeding 10 inches triggers violation
Sidewalks must be kept clear and in good repair by property owners
Snow and ice removed from sidewalks within 12 hours of storm ending
No accumulation of junk, debris, or garbage on private property
Commercial storefronts must maintain clean and presentable facades
Vacant properties must be registered and maintained

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Dogs must be licensed and controlled; the city operates an animal control program.
keyRules: All dogs over 7 months must be licensed annually with the City Clerk
Rabies vaccination mandatory for all dogs and cats
Dogs must be leashed when on public property or off owner's premises
Pet waste must be picked up immediately from public areas
Maximum number of pets per household may apply — check local ordinance
Aggressive or dangerous dogs subject to additional regulations

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: Parking enforcement is active in the city's busy downtown and commercial areas.
keyRules: Metered parking enforced Monday-Saturday, typically 8:00 AM to 6:00 PM
Two-hour limits in many commercial zones without feeding meters
Overnight street parking may require a residential permit in certain areas
No double parking — strictly enforced on Main Street and adjacent blocks
Snow emergency parking restrictions in effect during declared emergencies

rentals.title: Rental Regulations
rentals.summary: Hackensack has a significant rental population and comprehensive landlord-tenant regulations.
keyRules: All rental units must be registered with the city
Certificate of Habitability / Occupancy required for all rental units
Landlords must maintain units to housing code standards
NJ Anti-Eviction Act protections apply to tenants
Security deposit limited to 1.5 months' rent under state law
PILOT-agreement buildings may have separate regulatory requirements
rentals.source: Hackensack City Code, NJ Landlord-Tenant Law

other.title: Redevelopment Zone Regulations
other.summary: Several areas of Hackensack are designated as redevelopment zones with specific requirements.
keyRules: Developers must comply with adopted Redevelopment Plans
PILOT agreements negotiated for qualifying projects
Design standards and density requirements specific to each zone
Public amenity contributions may be required as part of approvals

other.title: Food Establishment Regulations
other.summary: Restaurants and food establishments are regulated by the city Health Department.
keyRules: Health permits required for all food establishments
Regular inspections conducted by the Health Department
Outdoor dining permits available for qualifying restaurants
Food truck operations require specific permits and approved locations
other.source: Hackensack City Code, Bergen County Health Department

publicSchools.name: Hackensack High School
publicSchools.notes: Comprehensive high school offering AP courses, vocational programs, and strong athletics. Known as the Comets.

publicSchools.name: Hackensack Middle School
publicSchools.notes: Serves all of the city's middle school students with academic and enrichment programs

privateSchools.name: Holy Trinity School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic elementary school with a strong academic and faith-based curriculum

privateSchools.name: Bergen County Academies
privateSchools.notes: Bergen County's prestigious magnet high school located in Hackensack, offering specialized academies in STEM, arts, business, and culinary arts. Admission by competitive exam.

whyMoveHere: Bergen County seat with robust government, commercial, and healthcare infrastructure
Significant downtown revitalization creating a walkable, urban lifestyle in the suburbs
Relative affordability compared to surrounding Bergen County towns
Excellent access to Routes 4, 17, 80, and the Garden State Parkway
Home to Bergen County Academies, one of NJ's top public magnet high schools

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bus to Port Authority Bus Terminal
commuteToNYC.trainStation: Anderson Street Station (Pascack Valley Line) or Essex Street Station
commuteToNYC.notes: NJ Transit buses provide direct service from Hackensack to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Midtown. The Pascack Valley Line has two stations in Hackensack (Anderson Street and Essex Street) serving Hoboken Terminal, with connections to the PATH train to Manhattan. Bus service is generally faster for Midtown destinations.

costOfLiving.summary: Hackensack offers a more affordable entry point to Bergen County compared to many surrounding towns, while still providing urban amenities and good access to NYC.

pros: More affordable than many Bergen County towns with strong amenities
Walkable downtown with new restaurants, shops, and apartments
Excellent highway access to NYC, North Jersey, and the GWB
Bergen County Academies offers a world-class magnet school option
Hackensack University Medical Center provides top-tier healthcare nearby
cons: Public school ratings are lower than surrounding affluent suburbs
Urban density and traffic can be heavy, especially near Routes 4 and 17
Property taxes are relatively high for the home prices
Some neighborhoods still in transition from revitalization efforts
Limited green space compared to western Bergen County towns

restaurants.name: Solari's Restaurant
restaurants.description: A Hackensack institution since 1938 — classic Italian-American dining with red-sauce favorites, generous portions, and old-school charm.

restaurants.name: White Manna
restaurants.description: Legendary tiny diner famous for its sliders — small, juicy hamburgers grilled on a vintage griddle. A North Jersey bucket-list food destination since the 1940s.

restaurants.name: Amami Shima
restaurants.description: Authentic Japanese restaurant offering sushi, sashimi, and traditional cooked dishes in a refined setting.

restaurants.name: Guerriero's
restaurants.description: Neighborhood Italian restaurant and pizzeria known for brick-oven pizza, homemade pasta, and a welcoming family atmosphere.

restaurants.name: The Urban Tot
restaurants.description: Trendy New American restaurant and bar in the revitalized downtown, offering creative cocktails and a seasonally inspired menu.

parks.name: Hackensack River County Park
parks.description: A scenic Bergen County park along the Hackensack River with walking trails, a boat launch, playgrounds, and athletic fields.

parks.name: Borg's Woods Nature Preserve
parks.description: A rare old-growth forest preserve in the middle of the city, home to towering tulip trees and a variety of wildlife. A National Natural Landmark.
parks.address: Near Summit Avenue and Prospect Avenue, Hackensack, NJ 07601

parks.name: Columbus Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with a playground, walking paths, and open green space. A gathering spot for local families.
parks.address: Berry Street and First Street, Hackensack, NJ 07601

parks.name: Staib Park
parks.description: Community park offering sports facilities including tennis courts, basketball courts, and a running track.

events.name: Hackensack Street Fair
events.description: A major street fair along Main Street featuring vendors, live entertainment, food trucks, and family activities. Draws thousands of visitors.

events.name: Downtown Hackensack Restaurant Week
events.description: Participating downtown restaurants offer prix fixe menus and special deals, showcasing the city's diverse culinary scene.

events.name: Hackensack Holiday Celebration
events.description: Annual holiday festivities including tree lighting, menorah lighting, live music, and seasonal activities in the downtown area.

events.name: Bergen County Jazz Fest
events.description: Free jazz performances at venues throughout downtown Hackensack, celebrating the area's musical heritage.

attractions.name: Bergen County Courthouse
attractions.description: A grand Beaux-Arts courthouse built in 1912, serving as the architectural centerpiece of downtown Hackensack and the seat of Bergen County government.

attractions.name: Hackensack University Medical Center
attractions.description: One of New Jersey's largest and most respected hospitals, home to cutting-edge medical research and comprehensive healthcare services. A major regional employer.

attractions.name: USS Ling Submarine (NJ Naval Museum)
attractions.description: A World War II-era Balao-class submarine permanently docked along the Hackensack River, open for tours (subject to restoration status). A unique historical attraction.
attractions.address: Court Street and River Street, Hackensack, NJ 07601

shoppingAreas: Main Street Downtown — Revitalized commercial corridor with restaurants, shops, cafes, and professional services
Hackensack River Street area — Emerging mixed-use district with new retail and residential
Route 4 / Route 17 Corridors — Major retail corridors with big-box stores, malls, and auto dealerships nearby
The Shops at Riverside — Large retail complex on Route 4 in neighboring Hackensack/River Edge area

government.meetingSchedule: City Council meets on the first and third Tuesday of each month at 8:00 PM
government.meetingLocation: Hackensack City Hall, Council Chambers, 65 Central Avenue, Hackensack, NJ 07601

hospitals.name: Hackensack Meridian Health Palisades Medical Center
Valley Hospital
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450
